title: ThreadInfo Object
description: This class defines the methods and properties that define the type and size of a thread. This object is used to create and query thread and tapped (straight and tapered) hole features. A new ThreadInfo object is created by using the ThreadInfo.create method. If the ThreadInfo object is obtained from an existing thread or hole feature, modifying the ThreadInfo object will modify the feature.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: create
methods.description: This method creates a new ThreadInfo object that can be used to create a thread or tapped-hole feature. The ThreadInfo object defines the type and size of the thread to create. When creating a thread, the type and size of the thread are defined by specifying the thread type, designation, and class. Fusion uses this information to look up the full details of the thread in tables delivered with Fusion. The ThreadDataQuery object can be used to determine valid input for this information.   The thread type implicitly defines if the thread is standard or tapered. Tapered threads can only be used when creating tapped holes and are not supported for thread features.

methods.name: redefine
methods.description: Method that redefines an existing ThreadInfo object. This is typically used to change the thread of an existing thread or tapped hole.   The ThreadInfo object defines the type and size of a thread by specifying the thread type, designation, and class. Fusion uses this information to look up the full details of the thread in tables delivered with Fusion. The ThreadDataQuery object can be used to determine valid input for this information.   Tapered threads can only be used when creating or editing tapped holes and are not supported for thread features.

properties.name: isInternal
properties.description: Returns and sets if the thread is an internal or external thread. A value of true indicates an internal thread. It defaults to true.

properties.name: isRightHanded
properties.description: Gets and sets if the thread is right or left-handed thread. A value of true indicates a right-handed thread. It defaults to true.

properties.name: isTapered
properties.description: Indicates if this ThreadInfo object defines a standard or tapered thread.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: majorDiameter
properties.description: Returns the value that defines the major diameter. The units are centimeters.

properties.name: minorDiameter
properties.description: Returns the value that defines the minor diameter. The units are centimeters.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

properties.name: pitchDiameter
properties.description: Returns the value that defines the pitch diameter. The units are centimeters.

properties.name: taperAngle
properties.description: Returns the angle of the tapered thread in centimeters.   This is only valid when isTapered is true.

properties.name: taperTapDrillDiameter
properties.description: Returns the Diameter of the tap drill required to create this tap.   This is only valid when isTapered is true.

properties.name: taperThreadHeight
properties.description: Returns the height of a tapered thread in centimeters.   This is only valid when isTapered is true.

properties.name: taperUsefulThreadLength
properties.description: Returns the useful length of threads for a tapered thread in centimeters.   This is only valid when isTapered is true.

properties.name: taperWrenchMakeupInternalDiameter
properties.description: The wrench makeup internal diameter for a taper pipe thread, also known as the effective thread diameter, is the diameter at the point where the thread engagement occurs when the pipe is tightened with a wrench.   This is only valid when isTapered is true.

properties.name: threadAngle
properties.description: Returns the value that defines the thread angle. The units are degrees.

properties.name: threadClass
properties.description: Returns and sets the string that defines the thread class.

properties.name: threadDesignation
properties.description: Returns and sets the string that defines the thread designation.

properties.name: threadPitch
properties.description: Returns the value that defines the thread pitch. The units are centimeters.

properties.name: threadSize
properties.description: Returns the string that defines the thread size.

properties.name: threadType
properties.description: Returns and sets the string that defines the thread type.

related_samples.name: Thread Feature API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating a new thread feature.

classType.title: ThreadInfo.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

syntax.python: returnValue = adsk.fusion.ThreadInfo.classType()
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = adsk::fusion::ThreadInfo::classType();

classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

create.title: ThreadInfo.create Method
create.description: This method creates a new ThreadInfo object that can be used to create a thread or tapped-hole feature. The ThreadInfo object defines the type and size of the thread to create. When creating a thread, the type and size of the thread are defined by specifying the thread type, designation, and class. Fusion uses this information to look up the full details of the thread in tables delivered with Fusion. The ThreadDataQuery object can be used to determine valid input for this information. The thread type implicitly defines if the thread is standard or tapered. Tapered threads can only be used when creating tapped holes and are not supported for thread features.

syntax.python: returnValue = adsk.fusion.ThreadInfo.create(isTapered, isInternal, threadType, threadDesignation, threadClass, isRightHanded)
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = adsk::fusion::ThreadInfo::create(isTapered, isInternal, threadType, threadDesignation, threadClass, isRightHanded);

create.return_description: Returns the newly created ThreadInfo object or null if the creation failed.

parameters.name: isTapered
parameters.description: Input Boolean that indicates if the thread is straight or tapered.

parameters.name: isInternal
parameters.description: Input Boolean that indicates if the thread is internal or external. A value of true indicates an internal thread. When the ThreadInfo is used to create a tapped hole, this value is ignored since it is always an internal thread.

parameters.name: threadType
parameters.description: Input string that defines the thread type.

parameters.name: threadDesignation
parameters.description: Input string that contains the thread designation.

parameters.name: threadClass
parameters.description: Input string that defines the thread class. This argument is ignored for tapered threads, so an empty string can be used.

parameters.name: isRightHanded
parameters.description: Input boolean that defines if the thread is right or left-handed.

redefine.title: ThreadInfo.redefine Method
redefine.description: Method that redefines an existing ThreadInfo object. This is typically used to change the thread of an existing thread or tapped hole. The ThreadInfo object defines the type and size of a thread by specifying the thread type, designation, and class. Fusion uses this information to look up the full details of the thread in tables delivered with Fusion. The ThreadDataQuery object can be used to determine valid input for this information. Tapered threads can only be used when creating or editing tapped holes and are not supported for thread features.

syntax.python: returnValue = threadInfo_var.redefine(isTapered, isInternal, threadType, threadDesignation, threadClass, isRightHanded)
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = threadInfo_var->redefine(isTapered, isInternal, threadType, threadDesignation, threadClass, isRightHanded);

redefine.return_description: Returns true if the redefinition was successful.

parameters.name: isTapered
parameters.description: Input Boolean that indicates if the thread is straight or tapered.

parameters.name: isInternal
parameters.description: Input Boolean that indicates if the thread is internal or external. A value of true indicates an internal thread. This value is ignored when the ThreadInfo is used for a tapped hole since they are always internal.

parameters.name: threadType
parameters.description: Input string that defines the thread type.

parameters.name: threadDesignation
parameters.description: Input string that defines the thread designation.

parameters.name: threadClass
parameters.description: Input string that defines the thread class. This argument is ignored for tapered threads.

parameters.name: isRightHanded
parameters.description: Input Boolean that specifies if the thread is straight or tapered.
